subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Configure Python 3.10+ environment and install all core libraries required for the SOTA pipeline.

subsections.title: Participants
content: Familiar with Python virtual environment management (venv or conda)
Familiar with pip and other package management tools, including handling complex peer dependencies

subsections.title: Input
content: Contains all required Python packages, especially unstructured and its additional dependencies for PDF parsing
# LLM and Embeddings (using Ollama and open-source models as examples)
unstructured[pdf] # Key: [pdf] includes PDF parsing dependencies

subsections.title: Output
content: Output Description: An isolated Python environment containing all SOTA dependencies. Note: unstructured[pdf] may require additional system-level dependencies such as poppler-utils and tesseract-ocr.

steps.title: SOTA Document Parsing Layer: Layout-Aware Parsing

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use the unstructured.io library to perform deep layout-aware parsing on input PDF documents, converting them into structured semantic elements.

subsections.title: Participants
content: Understand the impact of different parsing strategies (fast vs hi_res) on performance and accuracy

subsections.title: Input
content: Input Description: Complex semi-structured PDF containing text, tables, and multi-level headings

subsections.title: Output
content: Output Name: Structured Document Element List (List of unstructured.documents.elements.Element)
Output Description: A Python list where each element is classified as Title, NarrativeText, Table, etc., laying the foundation for subsequent semantic chunking and retrieval.

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Define a strictly corresponding Python BaseModel class with nested models using Pydantic based on the target API documentation.

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role: AI Algorithm Engineer / Backend Engineer
Able to translate JSON schema into BaseModel, including Field definitions, type hints (typing.List, typing.bool), and nested models

subsections.title: Input
content: Input Description: Detailed schema of the target JSON output, including all keys, value types, and nested structures
info_introduction: bool = Field(..., description="Whether the document contains 'Abstract' section")
info_method: bool = Field(..., description="Whether the document contains 'Implementation Method' section")
info_dataset: bool = Field(..., description="Whether the document contains 'Data Introduction' section")
info_settings: bool = Field(..., description="Whether the document contains 'Implementation Configuration' section")

subsections.title: Output
content: Output Name: DocumentCheckResult Pydantic Model (Python Class)
Output Description: A Python class that can be directly called by LangChain's with_structured_output to enforce LLM output compliance with JSON

steps.title: Build and Execute E2E Intelligent Processing Chain

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use LangChain Expression Language (LCEL) to orchestrate a complete RAG pipeline: Parser -> Chunker -> Retriever -> Prompt -> Base Model -> Structured Output Parser, to automatically process PDF and generate target JSON.

subsections.title: Participants
content: Proficient in LangChain (LCEL), understanding RunnableParallel and RunnablePassthrough
Familiar with using with_structured_output and able to bind it with Pydantic models.
Advanced Prompt Engineering capabilities, able to write complex instructions to guide LLM to complete multiple tasks simultaneously (classification, summarization, extraction).

subsections.title: Input
content: Input Name: PDF File Storage Path, Pydantic Model, LLM Instance
Input Description: All outputs from steps 1, 2, and 3, plus an initialized LangChain LLM instance (such as a ChatOllama instance pointing to a local Qwen model).

subsections.title: Output
content: Output Name: Compliant Pydantic Object (serializable to JSON)
Output Description: Structured data that is 100% schema-compliant, generated by the SOTA RAG pipeline after complete analysis of the input document, ready for API response.
